_about: Company relations extracted from braindb wikis/facts (a side project), then LLM-classified into 4 lean types. Direction encodes roles (supplier->customer); no redundant inverse types. This file is the hand-off braindb -> Prometheux.
_pipeline: braindb facts  ->  SQL export (data/braindb_export.sql)  ->  LLM classification (data/facts.json)  ->  this graph  ->  Prometheux reasoning (prometheux/reason.py)

relations.fact: Chevron signed a 20-year power-purchase agreement to fuel a Microsoft gas-powered AI data center in West Texas.
Chevron is building a power plant to supply natural-gas power for the AI data-center buildout.
Google Cloud supplies HSBC under a multi-year AI partnership announced 2026-06-17.
Microsoft is building one of the largest gas-powered AI data-center projects in the US.
Google among Big Tech investing $750bn in AI infrastructure this year.
Meta investing hundreds of billions in AI infrastructure.
Amazon among Big Tech investing in AI infrastructure.
NVIDIA GPUs power the AI data-center buildout.
AMD data-center chips serve the AI buildout.
NVIDIA and AMD compete in data-center accelerators.
AMD and Intel compete in CPUs.
NVIDIA and Intel compete in AI/accelerated computing.
AMD and Micron co-occur across the semiconductor supply chain.
NVIDIA and Qualcomm co-occur as AI investment alternatives.
BioArctic AB entered a research & collaboration agreement with Eli Lilly and Company.
Analysts list Nvidia and SpaceX among investment alternatives for AI/space exposure.
Nvidia and Alphabet listed together as AI investment alternatives.
West Texas gas fields supply the natural gas Chevron uses for its power agreements.
Smart Sand (SND) produces silica sand used as proppants for hydraulic fracturing in oil & gas drilling.
Oil & gas drilling produces the natural gas increasingly used to power AI data centers.
Smart Sand faces structural headwinds from in-basin sand competition, compressing margins from $21 to ~$13-15/ton.
